title: ajuga pygmaea
intro: Ajuga pygmaea, also known as dwarf bugle or pygmy bugle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, with its leaves and roots exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The medicinal properties of Ajuga pygmaea are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, relieving menstrual cramps, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Ajuga pygmaea can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by drying the leaves to create a dried herb for use in capsules or tinctures.
